content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
August 25, 1987 
Page E. Bigelow 
Institute of Public Administration 
56 West 44th street 
New York, NY 
10036 
Dear Ms. Bigelow: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. G-87-208 
I am writing in response to your request for copies of the 
California Political Reform Act (the "Act"), disclosure forms, 
citations to court decisions, and training materials regarding 
t.he Act. 
I have enclosed copies of the Act, the required forms, 
training materials, and our casebook's table of contents, which 
lists among other things California cases that interpret the 
Act. 
The Fair Political Practices Commission's policy is to 
charge 10¢ a page for documents over ten pages. 
Consequently, 
we are billing you a total of $1.90 for a copy of the table of 
contents. 
Feel free to call or write if you need more information. 
DMG:MA:plh 
Enclosures 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel

INSTITUTE OF PUBLIC' ADMINISTRATION

t 


California 
Fair Political 
'Practices Commission 
Page Elizabeth Bigelow 
55 West 44th street 
New York, New York 10036 
Dear Ms. Bigelow: 
August 3, 1987 
Re: 
87-208 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on July 27, 1987 by the Fair Political 
Practices commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact Margarita Altamirano, an 
attorney in the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to information needed. 
If your request is for 
informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
DMG:jaj 
Very truly yours, 
Chc~,. h. {j/ N;CL 
Diane M. Griffiths 
~-­
General Counsel 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • (916)322,5660

sections.question_synthetic: Ms. Bigelow requested copies of the California Political Reform Act, disclosure forms, citations to court decisions, and training materials regarding the Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC provided the requested materials including copies of the Act, required forms, training materials, and a table of contents from their casebook listing California cases interpreting the Act, with a billing of $1.90 for documents over ten pages at 10¢ per page.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document consists of two letters (acknowledgment and substantive response) rather than a single advice letter with traditional Q/A sections. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. The substantive response is informal in nature, providing materials and information rather than legal guidance on a specific compliance question.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC provided the requested materials including copies of the Act, required forms, training materials, and a table of contents from their casebook listing California cases interpreting the Act, with a billing of $1.90 for documents over ten pages at 10¢ per page.

embedding.summary: This is a two-part correspondence from the FPPC to Ms. Bigelow: an initial acknowledgment letter (August 3, 1987) confirming receipt of her advice request and outlining response timelines, followed by a substantive response letter (August 25, 1987) providing the requested materials on the Political Reform Act and related documents.
